title: Write an Oban worker test that uses perform_job with setup data
prompt: Write `test/my_app/workers/user_notification_worker_test.exs` that: (1) `use MyApp.DataCase, async: true`, (2) `use Oban.Testing, repo: MyApp.Repo`, (3) creates a user in the test via `Accounts.create_user/1`, (4) calls `assert :ok = perform_job(MyApp.Workers.UserNotificationWorker, %{"user_id" => user.id})`, (5) asserts a downstream effect (e.g., `Notifications.count_for(user.id) == 1`). The key point: `perform_job` runs in the test process, so it sees the sandbox data.
tier_rationale: Pattern combining DataCase + Oban.Testing + string-keyed args.
